scenario: Vanta Payments, a fintech running a Camunda 8.8 proof of concept for 10000 transactions per day, plans to ship Operate alongside Zeebe. The architect needs to confirm exactly where Operate may legally run before the team signs the rollout plan for production clusters against the Camunda 8 commercial offering.
question: Which statement correctly describes how Operate is licensed and made available with Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Operate is licensed only under a paid Enterprise subscription and cannot be used in any environment without first signing a full Camunda 8 commercial plan.
Operate is offered under the Operate trial license for use in non-production environments only, with production use available through the Camunda 8 offering.
Operate is freely licensed for both production and non-production use through a perpetual open-source community license shipped together with each Zeebe broker.
Operate is available only as a 30-day evaluation under the trial license, after which non-production access is also blocked until the cluster is upgraded to a paid plan.

a.text: Incorrect. The Operate trial license explicitly permits use without a paid plan, but only for non-production environments; a paid Camunda 8 plan is required for production use, not for any use at all.

b.text: Correct. The docs state that Operate is available under the Operate trial license with no time or feature restrictions as long as it is used in non-production environments, and is available for production use with support in the Camunda 8 offering.

c.text: Incorrect. Operate is not shipped under a perpetual open-source community license; it is offered under the Operate trial license for non-production use and through the commercial Camunda 8 offering for production.

d.text: Incorrect. The Operate trial license places no restriction on the length of the evaluation period or on the available feature set, so a 30-day expiry on non-production use does not apply.

explanation: Operate is offered under the Operate trial license for non-production use with no time or feature limits, and is available for production use with support as part of the Camunda 8 commercial offering.
